title: Gov Dashboards
text: This site contains reproducible dashboards built from government/statistical sources.

title: Gov Dashboards
text:
Framework contract
Each indicator follows the same pipeline:

API pull → raw tibble
Parse/clean → standardized tibble: date, value, series_id, label, freq
Visualization → modular plot function returning a ggplot (optionally plotly)

title: Gov Dashboards
text:
Notes

Static charts by default (ggplot).
Interactive charts are opt-in (plotly), controlled per dashboard.

title: Labor Market
text:
Code
# ── Source all required R modules ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 00_config.R    → shared package loads (dplyr, ggplot2, etc.)
# 01_helpers.R   → caching and environment variable utilities
# API_bls.R      → raw BLS API connector
# mock_data.R    → synthetic data used when USE_MOCK = TRUE
# parse_cpi.R    → provides parse_bls_series() (shared parser, sourced first)
# parse_labor.R  → get_labor_bundle() wrapper
# viz_timeseries.R → maybe_interactive() wrapper for plotly
# viz_labor.R    → plot_unrate(), plot_unrate_forecast()
# forecast_nextq.R → forecast_next_quarter() ARIMA model

source("R/00_config.R")
source("R/01_helpers.R")
source("R/API_bls.R")
source("R/mock_data.R")
source("R/parse_cpi.R")      # provides parse_bls_series() used by parse_labor.R
source("R/parse_labor.R")
source("R/viz_timeseries.R")
source("R/viz_labor.R")
source("R/forecast_nextq.R")

# ── Mock vs. live data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USE_MOCK auto-detects whether BLS_API_KEY is available.
# Locally: set BLS_API_KEY in ~/.Renviron
# In CI:   set BLS_API_KEY in GitHub Secrets
USE_MOCK    <- Sys.getenv("BLS_API_KEY") == ""
INTERACTIVE <- FALSE   # flip to TRUE for plotly hover/zoom

title: Labor Market
text:
Unemployment Rate (Full History)


Code
# Fetch (or mock) the U-3 Unemployment Rate, Seasonally Adjusted.
# Output: standard tibble — date, value (%), series_id, label, freq
labor <- get_labor_bundle(
  start_year = 2000,
  use_mock   = USE_MOCK,
  use_cache  = TRUE,
  cache      = TRUE
)




Code
# Chart 1: Full unemployment rate history with NBER recession shading.
# Grey bands mark officially designated recession periods.
# To use this chart alone in a presentation: copy just this chunk.
p_unrate <- plot_unrate(labor)
maybe_interactive(p_unrate, INTERACTIVE)

title: Labor Market
text:
3-Month Unemployment Forecast


Code
# Chart 2: Recent unemployment + ARIMA model forecast for the next 3 months.
# history_n = 60 shows the last 5 years for readability.
# Shaded bands represent 80% and 95% confidence intervals.
# To use this chart alone in a presentation: copy just this chunk.
p_unrate_forecast <- plot_unrate_forecast(labor, history_n = 60)
maybe_interactive(p_unrate_forecast, INTERACTIVE)
















Note



Data source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), Current Population Survey (CPS). U-3 Unemployment Rate, Seasonally Adjusted (series LNS14000000). Released monthly on the first Friday of the following month. Recession shading based on NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee designations. Forecast produced by an ARIMA model; confidence intervals reflect model uncertainty only.

title: GDP
text:
Code
# ── Source all required R modules ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 00_config.R    → shared package loads (dplyr, ggplot2, etc.)
# 01_helpers.R   → caching and environment variable utilities
# API_bea.R      → raw BEA API connector
# mock_data.R    → synthetic data used when USE_MOCK = TRUE
# parse_gdp.R    → parse_bea_gdp() + get_gdp_bundle()
# viz_timeseries.R → maybe_interactive() wrapper for plotly
# viz_gdp.R      → plot_gdp_growth(), plot_gdp_forecast()
# forecast_nextq.R → forecast_next_quarter() ARIMA model

source("R/00_config.R")
source("R/01_helpers.R")
source("R/API_bea.R")
source("R/mock_data.R")
source("R/parse_gdp.R")
source("R/viz_timeseries.R")
source("R/viz_gdp.R")
source("R/forecast_nextq.R")

# ── Mock vs. live data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USE_MOCK auto-detects whether BEA_API_KEY is available.
# Locally: set BEA_API_KEY in ~/.Renviron
# In CI:   set BEA_API_KEY in GitHub Secrets
USE_MOCK    <- Sys.getenv("BEA_API_KEY") == ""
INTERACTIVE <- FALSE   # flip to TRUE for plotly hover/zoom

title: GDP
text:
Quarterly GDP Growth (Full History)


Code
# Fetch (or mock) real GDP growth — annualized % change from prior quarter.
# Output: standard tibble — date (first month of quarter), value (%), series_id,
#         label, freq = "Q"
gdp <- get_gdp_bundle(
  start_year = 2000,
  use_mock   = USE_MOCK,
  use_cache  = TRUE,
  cache      = TRUE
)




Code
# Chart 1: Bar chart of annualized GDP growth by quarter.
# Green = expansion, red/pink = contraction.
# COVID collapse (Q2 2020) and rebound (Q3 2020) are clearly visible.
# To use this chart alone in a presentation: copy just this chunk.
p_gdp_growth <- plot_gdp_growth(gdp)
maybe_interactive(p_gdp_growth, INTERACTIVE)

title: GDP
text:
3-Quarter GDP Forecast


Code
# Chart 2: Recent GDP quarters + ARIMA model forecast for the next 3 quarters.
# history_n = 20 shows the last 5 years (20 quarters) for context.
# Shaded bands represent 80% and 95% confidence intervals.
# To use this chart alone in a presentation: copy just this chunk.
p_gdp_forecast <- plot_gdp_forecast(gdp, history_n = 20)
maybe_interactive(p_gdp_forecast, INTERACTIVE)
















Note



Data source: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), National Income and Product Accounts (NIPA) Table 1.1.1 — Percent Change from Preceding Period in Real Gross Domestic Product (BEA series A191RL). Seasonally Adjusted Annual Rate (SAAR). Advance estimate released ~4 weeks after quarter-end; preliminary and final revisions follow in subsequent months. Forecast produced by an ARIMA model; confidence intervals reflect model uncertainty only and should not be interpreted as policy projections.

title: Inflation
text:
Code
# ── Source all required R modules ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 00_config.R    → shared package loads (dplyr, ggplot2, etc.)
# 01_helpers.R   → caching and environment variable utilities
# API_bls.R      → raw BLS API connector (POST request, returns JSON)
# mock_data.R    → synthetic data used when USE_MOCK = TRUE
# parse_cpi.R    → cleans BLS JSON → standard tibble; provides get_cpi_bundle()
# viz_timeseries.R → maybe_interactive() wrapper for plotly
# viz_inflation.R → plot_cpi_index(), plot_cpi_yoy(), plot_cpi_forecast()
# forecast_nextq.R → forecast_next_quarter() ARIMA model

source("R/00_config.R")
source("R/01_helpers.R")
source("R/API_bls.R")
source("R/mock_data.R")
source("R/parse_cpi.R")
source("R/viz_timeseries.R")
source("R/viz_inflation.R")
source("R/forecast_nextq.R")

# ── Mock vs. live data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USE_MOCK auto-detects whether BLS_API_KEY is available.
# Locally: set BLS_API_KEY in ~/.Renviron
# In CI:   set BLS_API_KEY in GitHub Secrets
USE_MOCK    <- Sys.getenv("BLS_API_KEY") == ""
INTERACTIVE <- FALSE   # flip to TRUE for plotly hover/zoom

title: Inflation
text:
Price Index Level


Code
# Fetch (or mock) the CPI-U All Items, Seasonally Adjusted series.
# Output: standard tibble — date, value (index), series_id, label, freq
cpi <- get_cpi_bundle(
  start_year = 2000,
  use_mock   = USE_MOCK,
  use_cache  = TRUE,
  cache      = TRUE
)




Code
# Chart 1: Raw index level — shows cumulative price growth over time.
# To use this chart alone in a presentation: copy just this chunk.
p_cpi_index <- plot_cpi_index(cpi)
maybe_interactive(p_cpi_index, INTERACTIVE)

title: Inflation
text:
Year-over-Year Inflation Rate


Code
# Chart 2: YoY % change — the "headline inflation" number reported in the news.
# Dashed line marks the Fed's 2% inflation target.
# To use this chart alone in a presentation: copy just this chunk.
p_cpi_yoy <- plot_cpi_yoy(cpi)
maybe_interactive(p_cpi_yoy, INTERACTIVE)

title: Inflation
text:
3-Month Inflation Forecast


Code
# Chart 3: Recent YoY inflation + ARIMA model forecast for the next 3 months.
# Shaded bands represent 80% and 95% confidence intervals.
# history_n = 60 shows the last 5 years for readability.
# To use this chart alone in a presentation: copy just this chunk.
p_cpi_forecast <- plot_cpi_forecast(cpi, history_n = 60)
maybe_interactive(p_cpi_forecast, INTERACTIVE)
















Note



Data source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), CPI-U All Items Seasonally Adjusted (series CUSR0000SA0). Released monthly, typically in the second week of the following month. Forecast produced by an ARIMA model fit to the YoY % change series; confidence intervals reflect model uncertainty only and do not incorporate external shocks.

title: Interest Rates
text:
Code
# ── Source all required R modules ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Each file handles one responsibility:
#   00_config.R   → package loads (shared across all dashboards)
#   01_helpers.R  → caching utilities and small helper functions
#   API_fred.R    → raw FRED API connector (returns tibble)
#   parse_rates.R → cleans/labels raw FRED data; provides get_rates_bundle()
#   viz_timeseries.R → plot_timeseries(), maybe_interactive()
#   viz_yieldcurve.R → plot_spread_10y_2y()
#   forecast_nextq.R → forecast_next_quarter() with 80/95% confidence bands
#   mock_data.R   → realistic fake data used when USE_MOCK = TRUE

source("R/00_config.R")
source("R/01_helpers.R")
source("R/API_fred.R")
source("R/mock_data.R")
source("R/parse_rates.R")
source("R/viz_timeseries.R")
source("R/viz_yieldcurve.R")
source("R/forecast_nextq.R")

# ── Mock vs. live data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USE_MOCK auto-detects whether FRED_API_KEY is available.
# When running locally without a key, mock data is used automatically.
# In CI (GitHub Actions), FRED_API_KEY is injected via Secrets → live data used.
USE_MOCK     <- Sys.getenv("FRED_API_KEY") == ""
INTERACTIVE  <- FALSE   # flip to TRUE for plotly hover/zoom

title: Interest Rates
text:
Selected U.S. Interest Rates


Code
# Pull (or mock) all three rate series in one call.
# Output: standardized tibble with columns date, value, series_id, label, freq
rates <- get_rates_bundle(
  observation_start = "1990-01-01",
  use_mock          = USE_MOCK,
  use_cache         = TRUE,
  cache             = TRUE
)




Code
# Chart 1: All three series on one panel (Fed Funds, 2Y, 10Y Treasury)
# Use p_rates on its own in a presentation to show just this chart.
p_rates <- plot_timeseries(
  rates,
  title = "Selected U.S. Interest Rates (FRED)",
  ylab  = "Percent"
)
maybe_interactive(p_rates, INTERACTIVE)

title: Interest Rates
text:
10Y – 2Y Treasury Spread (Yield Curve Indicator)


Code
# Chart 2: Yield curve spread.
# Negative values (inversion) historically precede recessions.
# Use p_spread on its own in a presentation to show just this chart.
p_spread <- plot_spread_10y_2y(rates)
maybe_interactive(p_spread, INTERACTIVE)

title: Interest Rates
text:
Fed Funds Rate: 3-Month Forecast


Code
# Chart 3: ARIMA model fit to monthly Fed Funds Rate.
# Shaded bands: inner = 80% confidence interval, outer = 95%.
# Use p_ff_forecast on its own in a presentation to show just this chart.
ff <- rates |>
  dplyr::filter(series_id == "DFF") |>
  dplyr::select(date, value)

band <- forecast_next_quarter(ff)

p_ff_forecast <- ggplot(ff, aes(date, value)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, na.rm = TRUE, color = "#2c7bb6") +
  # 95% confidence band (lighter)
  geom_ribbon(
    data        = band,
    aes(x = date, ymin = lo95, ymax = hi95),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill        = "#2c7bb6",
    alpha       = 0.15
  ) +
  # 80% confidence band (darker)
  geom_ribbon(
    data        = band,
    aes(x = date, ymin = lo80, ymax = hi80),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill        = "#2c7bb6",
    alpha       = 0.25
  ) +
  # Forecast mean line
  geom_line(
    data        = band,
    aes(x = date, y = mean),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth   = 0.8,
    linetype    = "dashed",
    color       = "#d7191c"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Fed Funds Rate: Model-Based Forecast (Next 3 Months)",
    subtitle = "Shaded bands: 80% and 95% confidence intervals",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Percent"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

maybe_interactive(p_ff_forecast, INTERACTIVE)
